items.title: Anti-Trump protesters turn out to rallies in New York, Washington and other cities across country
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Protests against President Donald Trump’s administration are once again taking place in communities across the U.S. (AP video by Nathan Ellgren & Joseph B. Frederick)

<article>Pope Francis appeared in St Peter's Basilica on Saturday afternoon, to the surprise of the faithful present.

items.title: 277 Ukrainian soldiers returned in prisoner swap with Russia, Zelenskyy says
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Ukrainian President Zelenskyy said Saturday that 277 Ukrainian soldiers have been returned from Russian captivity in a prisoner exchange. 
Video shows dozens of released Ukrainian POWs cheering and greeting each other and their relatives at an undisclosed location. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Russia’s Ministry of Defense says 246 service members returned in prisoner exchange with Ukraine
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Russia’s Ministry of Defense said Saturday that 246 service members were returned from territory controlled by Kyiv, in a prisoner of war exchange with Ukraine. 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress

items.title: Thousands gather in London to support trans rights following ruling over definition of woman
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Thousands of trans rights protesters gathered in central London on Saturday, days after the U.K.'s Supreme Court ruled that a woman is someone born biologically female and that transgender women are excluded from that legal definition. (AP Video by Kwiyeon Ha)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Bodies of 15 victims, including children, killed in Israeli attacks, brought to Khan Younis hospital
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Families mourned their relatives in Khan Younis Saturday after Israeli strikes in Gaza killed more than 90 people in 48 hours, according to Gaza’s Health Ministry. The dead include 15 people who were killed overnight, among them women and children, some of whom were sheltering in a designated humanitarian zone, according to hospital staff.  (AP Video shot by Abdel Kareem Hana; Production by Wafaa Shurafa) 

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Russia’s Putin announces an Easter ceasefire in Ukraine
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Russian President Vladimir Putin announced a temporary Easter ceasefire in Ukraine, citing humanitarian reasons, the Kremlin said Saturday.
According to the Kremlin, the ceasefire will last from 6 p.m. Moscow time (1500 GMT) on Saturday to midnight (2100 GMT) following Easter Sunday.

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Where do whale sharks mate? The search to learn where the magic happens for the world's biggest fish
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Nobody knows where whale sharks are mating. But scientists suspect it may be happening in the waters around St. Helena. It's the only place in the world where adult male and female whale sharks are known to regularly gather in roughly equal numbers. (AP video by Maria Cheng/Sharon Johnson)(AP produced by Javier Arciga)

Subscribe: http://smarturl.it/AssociatedPress
Read more: https://apnews.com​

This video may be available for archive licensing via https://newsroom.ap.org/home</article>

items.title: Supreme Court to hear case concerning LGBTQ books in public schools
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Five children's books featuring LGBTQ characters, aimed at grades K through 5, have roiled a suburban Maryland school district and led to a Supreme Court case that the justices will hear on Tuesday. (AP Video: Mike Pesoli)

items.title: Steve Witkoff arrives at Oman embassy in Rome for second round of talks with Iran
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A convoy carrying U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff arrived at the Omani Embassy in Rome Saturday for the second round of talks with Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Aragchi over Tehran's rapidly advancing nuclear program. (AP Video/Andrea Rosa)

items.title: Some Jan. 6 rioters are now being embraced as heroes, potential candidates for office
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>The rioters who attempted to take over the U.S. Capitol on Jan. 6, 2021, are fielding new opportunities in the political arena after receiving pardons from President Donald Trump (AP video: Mike Householder)

items.title: LIVE: Vigil held on FSU campus after shooting
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>A vigil is held in front of the "Unconquered" statue after two people were killed and at least six others were wounded when a gunman opened fire at Florida State University. Police said a 20-year-old suspect — the son of a sheriff’s deputy — was shot and taken into custody.  

#fsu #news</article>

items.title: Firefighters battle blazes after Russian attacks in Ukraine's Odesa and Sumy regions
items.author: Associated Press
items.content:
<article>Russian attacks damaged farms in Ukraine's Odesa region and sparked fires in Sumy overnight, the Ukrainian emergency service said Saturday.
